title: Census-to-CoC crosswalk
code: nrow(tract_coc2019)                       # ~73k tracts assigned #> [1] 73666 length(unique(tract_coc2019$COCNUM))      # ~394 CoCs #> [1] 395 # tracts that split across more than one CoC (area-weighted file) sum(table(tract_coc_wt2019$GEOID) > 1) #> [1] 10168

what: Focus: a California and a Washington CoC
title: Census-to-CoC crosswalk
text: List counties make Seattle/King County (WA-500) much CoC falls , using area weights: tracts assigned San Francisco’s CoC (CA-501):
code: # use subset() (or which()): some tracts have NA COCNUM, and indexing a data # frame with a logical vector containing NA returns spurious NA rows. wa500 <- subset(county_coc2019, COCNUM == "WA-500") wa500[order(-wa500$w_coc), c("fips", "w_coc")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   fips     w_coc #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 53033 0.990    #> 2 53061 0.00411  #> 3 53053 0.00340  #> 4 53037 0.00225  #> 5 53007 0.000557 ca501 <- subset(tract_coc2019, COCNUM == "CA-501") head(ca501[, c("GEOID", "fips", "COCNAME")]) #>            GEOID  fips           COCNAME #> 9833 06075010100 06075 San Francisco CoC #> 9834 06075010200 06075 San Francisco CoC #> 9835 06075010300 06075 San Francisco CoC #> 9836 06075010400 06075 San Francisco CoC #> 9837 06075010500 06075 San Francisco CoC #> 9838 06075010600 06075 San Francisco CoC nrow(ca501) #> [1] 195

what: Aggregating an ACS variable up to a CoC
title: Census-to-CoC crosswalk
text: Pull tract-level American Community Survey variable aggregate CoCs using area weights. (Requires Census API key; run .)
code: library(tidycensus) library(dplyr) # tract population for Washington pop <- get_acs("tract", variables = "B01003_001", state = "WA",                year = 2019, survey = "acs5") tract_coc_wt2019 %>%   inner_join(pop, by = c("GEOID")) %>%   group_by(COCNUM, COCNAME) %>%   summarise(coc_population = sum(estimate * w_tract), .groups = "drop") %>%   arrange(desc(coc_population))

title: Census-to-CoC crosswalk
text: CoCs merge renumbered. Roll historical code forward surviving CoC coc_mergers:
code: head(coc_mergers) #>   coc_pre coc_post merger_year #> 1  MI-520   MI-500        2002 #> 2  TN-505   TN-503        2006 #> 3  KS-500   KS-507        2008 #> 4  MI-521   MI-500        2008 #> 5  MI-524   MI-500        2008 #> 6  MN-507   MN-503        2008 resolve_coc <- function(code, mergers = coc_mergers) {   repeat {     hit <- mergers$coc_post[match(code, mergers$coc_pre)]     if (is.na(hit) || hit == code) return(code)     code <- hit   } } resolve_coc("AR-502") #> [1] "AR-503"

title: CoC Point-in-Time homeless counts
text: National totals full series:  2021 dip reflects HUD’s pandemic waiver unsheltered count many CoCs, real decline.
code: years <- 2007:2025 totals <- sapply(years, function(y) sum(get(paste0("hud", y))$count)) plot(years, totals / 1e6, type = "b", pch = 19,      xlab = "Year", ylab = "Total PIT homeless (millions)",      main = "U.S. Point-in-Time homeless counts, 2007-2024")

what: Sheltered, unsheltered and total, with ARIMA reconstruction of 2021
title: CoC Point-in-Time homeless counts
text: pit_us splits national total sheltered unsheltered counts. 2021 unsheltered count collapsed waiver; sheltered count held . treat 2021 missing reconstruct series ARIMA model (Kalman smoothing via imputeTS::na_kalman), shown hollow points.  national map 2024, CoCs shaded PIT count. Alaska Hawaii repositioned tigris::shift_geometry() compact lower-48 layout:
code: library(ggplot2) series <- c("total", "sheltered", "unsheltered") yr <- pit_us$year # ARIMA-impute the 2021 value of one series (2021 set to NA) arima_2021 <- function(v) {   x <- v; x[yr == 2021] <- NA   as.numeric(imputeTS::na_kalman(ts(x, start = min(yr)), model = "auto.arima"))[yr == 2021] } obs <- do.call(rbind, lapply(series, function(s)   data.frame(year = yr, series = s, value = pit_us[[s]]))) imp <- data.frame(year = 2021, series = series,                   value = vapply(series, function(s) arima_2021(pit_us[[s]]), numeric(1)))  ggplot(obs, aes(year, value, color = series)) +   geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1.3) +   geom_point(data = imp, shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 3.2, stroke = 1.1) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(title = "U.S. PIT homeless by shelter status, 2007-2025",        subtitle = "Hollow points: ARIMA reconstruction of the waiver-affected 2021 count",        y = NULL, x = NULL, color = NULL) +   theme_minimal() data.frame(series, observed_2021 = sapply(series, function(s) pit_us[[s]][yr == 2021]),            arima_2021 = round(imp$value)) #>                  series observed_2021 arima_2021 #> total             total        379055     560133 #> sheltered     sheltered        325027     288340 #> unsheltered unsheltered         54028     227548 library(sf); library(ggplot2); library(tigris) #> Linking to GEOS 3.14.1, GDAL 3.12.3, PROJ 9.8.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE #> To enable caching of data, set `options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)` #> in your R script or .Rprofile. # drop the far Pacific/Caribbean territories (shift_geometry only repositions # AK/HI/PR) so the map stays centered on the lower 48 + insets conus <- function(x) x[!x$ST %in% c("AS", "GU", "MP", "VI"), ] m24 <- conus(merge(coc2024, hud2024, by.x = "COCNUM", by.y = "coc_num")) m24s <- shift_geometry(m24) ggplot(m24s) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = count), color = NA) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(trans = "log10", name = "PIT\nhomeless",                        labels = scales::comma) +   labs(title = "Point-in-Time homeless by CoC, 2024") +   theme_void()

what: A helper for the two state examples
title: CoC Point-in-Time homeless counts
code: # the 3 largest CoCs in a state in 2024, and a short label for the map top_cocs <- function(st, n = 3) {   s <- m24[m24$ST == st, ]   s <- s[order(-s$count), ][seq_len(n), ]   s$label <- paste0(s$COCNUM, "\n", substr(s$COCNAME, 1, 22))   s } # state map: all CoCs shaded by count, top 3 labeled by name state_map <- function(st, title) {   s <- m24[m24$ST == st, ]   tp <- top_cocs(st)   ggplot(s) +     geom_sf(aes(fill = count), color = "grey70") +     geom_sf_text(data = tp, aes(label = label), size = 2.6, fontface = "bold") +     scale_fill_viridis_c(trans = "log10", name = "PIT", labels = scales::comma) +     labs(title = title) + theme_void() } # per-CoC time series 2007-2024 for a set of CoC numbers coc_series <- function(coc_nums) {   do.call(rbind, lapply(coc_nums, function(cc) data.frame(     year = years, COCNUM = cc,     count = sapply(years, function(y) {       d <- get(paste0("hud", y)); v <- d$count[d$coc_num == cc]       if (length(v)) v else NA_real_ })))) }

title: CoC Point-in-Time homeless counts
text: Year--year trend California’s three largest CoCs:
code: state_map("CA", "California: PIT homeless by CoC, 2024 (top 3 labeled)") #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(sf::st_zm(x)): st_point_on_surface may not #> give correct results for longitude/latitude data ca_top <- top_cocs("CA") ggplot(coc_series(ca_top$COCNUM), aes(year, count, color = COCNUM)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) + geom_point(size = 1.3) +   scale_color_discrete(labels = setNames(substr(ca_top$COCNAME, 1, 28), ca_top$COCNUM)) +   labs(title = "California: largest CoCs, 2007-2024", y = "PIT homeless",        color = NULL) + theme_minimal()

title: CoC Point-in-Time homeless counts
text: Year--year trend Washington’s three largest CoCs:  CoCs merge renumbered time; use coc_mergers follow CoC merger (see ?coc_mergers).
code: state_map("WA", "Washington: PIT homeless by CoC, 2024 (top 3 labeled)") #> Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(sf::st_zm(x)): st_point_on_surface may not #> give correct results for longitude/latitude data wa_top <- top_cocs("WA") ggplot(coc_series(wa_top$COCNUM), aes(year, count, color = COCNUM)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) + geom_point(size = 1.3) +   scale_color_discrete(labels = setNames(substr(wa_top$COCNAME, 1, 28), wa_top$COCNUM)) +   labs(title = "Washington: largest CoCs, 2007-2024", y = "PIT homeless",        color = NULL) + theme_minimal()

what: Trends for the U.S., California and Washington
title: County-level homeless estimates
text: Total homeless per-capita rate, 2007–2025, summing county estimates region.
code: yrs_all <- 2007:2025 ycols <- sprintf("count%02d", yrs_all %% 100) regions <- list(US = homeless,                 California = homeless[homeless$state == "06", ],                 Washington = homeless[homeless$state == "53", ]) trend <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(regions), function(nm) {   h <- regions[[nm]]   data.frame(region = nm, year = yrs_all,              total = sapply(ycols, function(c) sum(h[[c]], na.rm = TRUE)),              pop   = sum(h$population, na.rm = TRUE)) })) trend$region <- factor(trend$region, c("US", "California", "Washington")) trend$per100k <- trend$total / trend$pop * 1e5  # total: faceted with free y-axes so CA/WA are legible next to the US ggplot(trend, aes(year, total)) +   geom_line(color = "steelblue") + geom_point(size = 1.2, color = "steelblue") +   facet_wrap(~ region, scales = "free_y") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(title = "Total homeless, 2007-2025", y = NULL, x = NULL) + theme_minimal() # per capita: one axis (rates are comparable), colored by region ggplot(trend, aes(year, per100k, color = region)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) + geom_point(size = 1.3) +   labs(title = "Homeless per 100,000, 2007-2025", y = "per 100k",        x = NULL, color = NULL) + theme_minimal()

what: County vs CoC: the same data at two resolutions
title: County-level homeless estimates
text: Disaggregating counties reveals spatial detail CoC areas mask. , 2024 totals shown first CoC level (administrative) county level (package’s estimate).   comparison California Washington:
code: coc24 <- shift_geometry(conus(merge(coc2024, hud2024, by.x = "COCNUM", by.y = "coc_num"))) coc24$count <- poslog(coc24$count) cty24 <- shift_geometry(sp_homeless); cty24$count <- poslog(sp_homeless$count24) ggplot(coc24) + geom_sf(aes(fill = count), color = NA) + fillc("PIT") +   labs(title = "United States, 2024 - CoC level") + theme_void() ggplot(cty24) + geom_sf(aes(fill = count), color = NA) + fillc("Est.") +   labs(title = "United States, 2024 - county level") + theme_void() state_pair <- function(st, fp) {   coc <- merge(coc2024, hud2024, by.x = "COCNUM", by.y = "coc_num")   coc <- coc[coc$ST == st, ]; coc$count <- poslog(coc$count)   sel <- substr(sp_homeless$fips, 1, 2) == fp   cty <- sp_homeless[sel, ]; cty$count <- poslog(sp_homeless$count24[sel])   print(ggplot(coc) + geom_sf(aes(fill = count), color = "grey70") + fillc("PIT") +           labs(title = paste(st, "2024 - CoC level")) + theme_void())   print(ggplot(cty) + geom_sf(aes(fill = count), color = "grey85") + fillc("Est.") +           labs(title = paste(st, "2024 - county level")) + theme_void()) } state_pair("CA", "06") state_pair("WA", "53")

title: County-level homeless estimates
text: Homeless per 100,000 residents. First county level:  CoC level (CoC count divided population counties):
code: yrs <- c(2007, 2010, 2020, 2024) cty_pc <- do.call(rbind, lapply(yrs, function(y) {   g <- shift_geometry(sp_homeless["population"])   g$rate <- poslog(sp_homeless[[ycol(y)]] / sp_homeless$population * 1e5)   g$year <- y; g[, c("year", "rate")] })) ggplot(cty_pc) + geom_sf(aes(fill = rate), color = NA) +   facet_wrap(~ year) + fillr() +   labs(title = "County homeless per 100,000 (log scale)") + theme_void() coc_pc <- do.call(rbind, lapply(yrs, function(y) {   coc <- get(paste0("coc", y)); hud <- get(paste0("hud", y))   pp <- coc_pop(y)   g <- merge(coc, merge(hud, pp, by.x = "coc_num", by.y = "COCNUM"),              by.x = "COCNUM", by.y = "coc_num")   g <- shift_geometry(conus(g))   g$rate <- poslog(g$count / g$population * 1e5)   g$year <- y; g[, c("year", "rate")] })) #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar ggplot(coc_pc) + geom_sf(aes(fill = rate), color = NA) +   facet_wrap(~ year) + fillr() +   labs(title = "CoC homeless per 100,000 (log scale)") + theme_void()

what: 2025 at a glance: CoC vs county, total vs per-capita
title: County-level homeless estimates
text: Four views latest year log scale.
code: library(patchwork)  # CoC layer with total count and per-capita rate coc25 <- merge(coc2025, hud2025, by.x = "COCNUM", by.y = "coc_num") coc25 <- merge(coc25, coc_pop(2025), by = "COCNUM") #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar coc25$rate <- coc25$count / coc25$population * 1e5 coc25 <- shift_geometry(conus(coc25)) coc25$c_l <- poslog(coc25$count); coc25$r_l <- poslog(coc25$rate)  # county layer cty25 <- shift_geometry(sp_homeless) cty25$c_l <- poslog(sp_homeless$count25) cty25$r_l <- poslog(sp_homeless$count25 / sp_homeless$population * 1e5)  panel <- function(g, col, sub) ggplot(g) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = .data[[col]]), color = NA) +   (if (grepl("100k", sub)) fillr() else fillc("count")) +   labs(subtitle = sub) + theme_void() +   theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(face = "bold"))  ((panel(coc25, "c_l", "CoC - total")     | panel(cty25, "c_l", "County - total")) /  (panel(coc25, "r_l", "CoC - per 100k")  | panel(cty25, "r_l", "County - per 100k"))) +   plot_annotation(title = "2025 homelessness: total and per-capita, CoC vs county")

title: County-level homeless estimates
code: cw <- substr(homeless$fips, 1, 2) %in% c("06", "53") imp <- is.na(homeless_na$count24[cw]) table(state = substr(homeless$fips[cw], 1, 2),       status = ifelse(imp, "imputed", "observed")) #>      status #> state observed #>    06       58 #>    53       39

what: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
title: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
text: Datasets spatial tools United States homelessness research. COCHomeless packages HUD Continuum Care (CoC) Point--Time (PIT) homeless counts, CoC boundaries, Census--CoC crosswalk, county-level homeless estimates, covering 2007–2025.

title: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
text: sf required (spatial objects). tidycensus, ggplot2, tigris, imputeTS others needed rebuild data run vignettes.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("SSDALab/COCHomeless", build_vignettes = TRUE) library(COCHomeless)

title: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
text: series run 2007 2025 (annual). 2025 PIT counts HUD’s official release; hud2025_prelim keeps earlier Community Solutions estimate reference. 2021 HUD waived unsheltered count many CoCs COVID-19, 2021 understates homelessness — vignettes show ARIMA reconstruction.

title: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
text: 1. CoC level (HUD administrative areas) 2. Census ↔︎ CoC crosswalk (links Census tracts/counties CoCs) Tract geography uses 2010-census tracts 2007–2019 2020-census tracts 2020–2025. 3. County-level estimates (CoC counts disaggregated counties)

title: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
text: installing build_vignettes = TRUE: CoC Point--Time homeless counts (coc-pit-counts) — national & state maps, per-CoC trends, sheltered/unsheltered series ARIMA reconstruction 2021. County-level homeless estimates (county-estimates) — county-vs-CoC comparison, per-capita maps, US/CA/WA trends, 2025 summary grid. Census--CoC crosswalk (census-coc-crosswalk) — using crosswalk coc_mergers. Vignette sources vignettes/; build scripts regenerate every dataset source data-raw/.
code: browseVignettes("COCHomeless")

title: Datasets and Spatial Tools for United States Homelessness Research
text: County estimates disaggregate CoC PIT counts population density impute counties CoC, following Almquist, Helwig & (2020). Please cite: Almquist, Z. W., Helwig, N. E., & , Y. (2020). Connecting Continuum Care point--time homeless counts United States Census areal units. Mathematical Population Studies, 27(1), 46–58. https://doi.org/10.1080/08898480.2019.1636574 Census--CoC crosswalk follows Tom Byrne’s HUD-CoC-Geography-Crosswalk; please credit using tract_coc* / county_coc* data. Run citation("COCHomeless") full details. ACS pulls need Census API key ~/.Renviron (CENSUS_API_KEY); never commit .

what: COCHomeless: Datasets and Spatial Tools for U.S. Homelessness Research — COCHomeless-package
title: COCHomeless: Datasets and Spatial Tools for U.S. Homelessness Research — COCHomeless-package
text: Provides HUD Continuum Care (CoC) Point--Time homeless counts, CoC geographic boundaries simple-feature objects, Census--CoC crosswalk linking census tracts counties CoCs, county-level homeless estimates American Community Survey covariates.

title: COCHomeless: Datasets and Spatial Tools for U.S. Homelessness Research — COCHomeless-package
text: CoC counts hud2007–hud2024 (preliminary     hud2025_prelim). CoC boundaries coc2007–coc2024, national sf. Census--CoC crosswalk tract_coc2019,     tract_coc_wt2019, county_coc2019 (2022). County estimates homeless, homeless_na,     sp_homeless, area.

title: COCHomeless: Datasets and Spatial Tools for U.S. Homelessness Research — COCHomeless-package
text: Cite package citation("COCHomeless"). Individual data types carry citations: county-level estimates follow Almquist, Helwig (2020) doi:10.1080/08898480.2019.1636574 ; data-source credits Census--CoC crosswalk given crosswalk help pages (tract_coc2019, county_coc2019).

title: COCHomeless: Datasets and Spatial Tools for U.S. Homelessness Research — COCHomeless-package
text: Maintainer: Zack W. Almquist zalmquist@uw.edu (ORCID) contributors: Yun [contributor] Tom Byrne (Census--CoC crosswalk procedure) [contributor]

title: CoC geographic boundaries, 2007-2025 — coc
text: HUD Continuum Care (CoC) boundary polygons year, coc2007 coc2024, national simple-feature (sf) objects one row per CoC. Built HUD's per-state CoC GIS shapefiles, combined nationally, CoC codes harmonized across vintages (embedded fiscal year "AL07-500" normalized "AL-500"). Geometries topology-preserving simplified (2 percent vertices retained) keep package manageable size; intended national-scale mapping areal analysis rather fine local cartography.

title: CoC geographic boundaries, 2007-2025 — coc
code: coc2007  coc2008  coc2009  coc2010  coc2011  coc2012  coc2013  coc2014  coc2015  coc2016  coc2017  coc2018  coc2019  coc2020  coc2021  coc2022  coc2023  coc2024  coc2025

text: sf data frame (CRS EPSG:4269, NAD83) one row per CoC   4 attributes plus geometry: COCNUM CoC number, e.g. "WA-500". COCNAME CoC name. ST Two-letter state/territory abbreviation. STATE_NAME State/territory name. geometry CoC boundary polygon. object class sf (inherits tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) 448 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 450 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 442 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 431 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 422 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 411 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 413 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 399 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 398 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 397 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) 393 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 398 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 389 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) 389 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 386 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) 387 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits data.frame) 387 rows 5 columns. object class sf (inherits tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) 388 rows 5 columns.

title: CoC geographic boundaries, 2007-2025 — coc
text: HUD CoC GIS tools:   https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/coc/gis-tools/

what: Continuum of Care (CoC) merger crosswalk over time — coc_mergers
title: Continuum of Care (CoC) merger crosswalk over time — coc_mergers
text: Records HUD Continuum Care (CoC) numbers reassigned CoCs merged. CoCs merge renumbered frequently, code appears early year hud20XX / coc20XX series may absorbed different CoC later. Use table harmonize CoC codes across years – example follow CoC's homeless counts merger, roll historical codes forward surviving CoC.

title: Continuum of Care (CoC) merger crosswalk over time — coc_mergers
code: coc_mergers

title: Continuum of Care (CoC) merger crosswalk over time — coc_mergers
text: data frame one row per merger 3 variables: coc_pre CoC number merger, e.g. "AR-502". coc_post Surviving CoC number merger, e.g. "AR-503". merger_year Year merger took effect (integer).

title: Continuum of Care (CoC) merger crosswalk over time — coc_mergers
text: "CoC Mergers" sheet HUD's PIT--CoC workbook (AHAR Part 1):   https://www.huduser.gov/portal/datasets/ahar/2024-ahar-part-1-pit-estimates--homelessness---us.html

title: Continuum of Care (CoC) merger crosswalk over time — coc_mergers
code: # Resolve any historical CoC code to its current (post-merger) code resolve_coc <- function(code, mergers = coc_mergers) {   repeat {     hit <- mergers$coc_post[match(code, mergers$coc_pre)]     if (is.na(hit) || hit == code) return(code)     code <- hit   } } resolve_coc("AR-502")  # -> "AR-503" #> [1] "AR-503"

title: U.S. county boundaries — counties
text: Cartographic boundaries (1:500,000) U.S. counties county equivalents, national sf object. Geometries simplified compact package size. Use base layer mapping county-level estimates (homeless) Census--CoC crosswalk.

title: U.S. county boundaries — counties
code: counties

title: U.S. county boundaries — counties
text: sf data frame (CRS EPSG:4269, NAD83) one row per   county 8 attributes plus geometry: fips 5-digit county FIPS code. STATEFP 2-digit state FIPS code. COUNTYFP 3-digit county FIPS code. NAME County name. NAMELSAD County name legal/statistical description. STUSPS Two-letter state abbreviation. STATE_NAME State name. ALAND Land area square meters. geometry County boundary polygon.

title: U.S. county boundaries — counties
text: U.S. Census Bureau TIGER/Line cartographic boundary files, via   tigris package (https://www.census.gov/geographies/mapping-files.html).

what: County to CoC area-weighted crosswalk — county_coc
title: County to CoC area-weighted crosswalk — county_coc
text: Area-weighted overlap U.S. counties HUD Continuum Care (CoC) boundaries, two normalizations supporting directions apportionment. Computed aggregating tract--CoC intersection county level equal-area projection (EPSG:5070).

what: County-level homeless estimates, 2007-2024 (missing values imputed) — homeless
title: County-level homeless estimates, 2007-2024 (missing values imputed) — homeless
text: County-level homeless counts missing values imputed, plus selected American Community Survey (ACS) demographic covariates. Counts produced disaggregating HUD Continuum Care (CoC) Point--Time counts counties using population-density weighting imputing counties CoC via spatial Poisson model, following Almquist, Helwig (2020) doi:10.1080/08898480.2019.1636574 . See homeless_na pre-imputation version.

title: County-level homeless estimates, 2007-2024 (missing values imputed) — homeless
text: Estimates bounded plausibility cap 2,000 homeless per 100,000 residents (2 percent population), 3,500 per 100,000 dense major-metro cores (New York City boroughs Los Angeles County). cap applied redistributing excess within CoC, CoC's Point--Time total preserved across counties. HUD CoC counts (hud2007) never altered (none exceed cap year).

what: Preliminary 2025 homelessness estimates (NOT official HUD data) — hud2025_prelim
title: Preliminary 2025 homelessness estimates (NOT official HUD data) — hud2025_prelim
text: Preliminary, directional estimates . National, category-level projections 2025 homelessness produced Community Solutions using population-weighted ratio estimator applied roughly 170 385 CoCs (69 percent 2024 Point--Time population) reported early. figures undergone HUD's quality-assurance process, Point--Time count, comparable official hud2007–hud2024 series. provided clearly flagged early indicator. HUD's official 2025 Point--Time counts now available hud2025 (national total 742,998); object retained reference pre-release estimate.

title: COCHomeless 0.2.0
text: Spatial data migrated sp sf. yearly Continuum Care boundary objects (coc2007–coc2024) now single national sf objects (one row per CoC) instead 51-element lists SpatialPolygonsDataFrames, sp_homeless now sf object. Imports now sf rather sp.

title: COCHomeless 0.2.0
text: Extended HUD Point--Time count series (hud2016–hud2024) CoC boundary series (coc2016–coc2024) 2024. Added Census--CoC crosswalk family: tract_coc20XX (hard tract--CoC assignment tract centroid) tract_coc_wt20XX (area- population-weighted tract/county--CoC overlap shares), adapted Tom Byrne’s crosswalk procedure. Added hud2025_prelim, clearly flagged preliminary 2025 estimate derived Community Solutions’ population-weighted ratio estimator. figures undergone HUD’s quality-assurance process directly comparable official hud20XX series.

title: COCHomeless 0.2.0
text: American Community Survey covariates now built tidycensus (former American FactFinder source retired). Added cross-sectional data-integrity tests three introductory vignettes centered California Washington. Documentation, DESCRIPTION, metadata updated CRAN compliance.
